<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after frames
after frames to see what changed
see what changed after clicking the red shape.

before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

# Check for changes in object properties
print("=== Changes detected ===")
for nid in after_nodes:
    if nid in before_nodes:
        bn = before_nodes[nid]
        an = after_nodes[nid]
        if bn['color'] != an['color']:
            print(f"ID {nid {nid}: color {bn['color']} -> {an['color']}")
        if bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
            print(f"ID {nid}: pixels {bn['pixels']} -> {an['pixels']}")
        if bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
            print(f"ID {nid}: boundary changed")
    else:
        print(f"ID {nid}: NEW object, color={after_nodes[nid]['color']}")

for nid in before_nodes:
    if nid not in after_nodes:
        print(f"ID {nid}: REMOVED, was color={before_nodes[nid]['color']}")

The key changes:
- ID 26 (red shape) -> became blue (b), pixels 40->16
- ID 27 (green shape) -> became red (R)
- ID 28 (purple) -> became light green (N), pixels 16->40
- ID 10 (charcoal background) -> light green (N), pixels 1962->59
- ID 11 (yellow) -> charcoal (c), pixels 4->1962
- Many other color swaps
This looks like clicking the red shape triggers a global color permutation. The colors are being cycled or swapped according to some rule.
Let me look at the pattern more carefully at the color changes to understand the permutation:
- N -> B (light green -> black
- c -> N (charcoal -> light green
- Y -> c (yellow -> charcoal

- p -> b (purple -> blue
- g -> S (gray -> sky
- G -> p (dark gray -> purple
- NEW G (dark gray)
This is very complex. Let me think about it differently. Maybe each click cycles colors in some pattern. Or maybe it's a specific permutation that depends on what was clicked.
